name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description: Project Layout is a Java utility library that detects, describes, and exposes conventional on-disk project directory layouts in a consistent, build-tool-aware way. The core abstraction is org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout, which models a project rooted at a filesystem directory and provides root-relative paths for production sources, test sources, and documentation roots, plus optional module discovery for multi-module repositories. Layout implementations include: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.MavenProjectLayout (reads pom.xml via org.machanism.machai.project.layout.PomReader to determine modules when packaging=pom and to derive source/test/resource directories using Maven build defaults when not explicitly set, and docs as src/site), org.machanism.machai.project.layout.GragleProjectLayout (uses the Gradle Tooling API to load a GradleProject model to enumerate child projects and returns conventional directories src/main, src/test, src/site), org.machanism.machai.project.layout.JScriptProjectLayout (parses package.json workspaces and resolves workspace modules by matching glob patterns to directories containing package.json; sources/tests/docs are not inferred and return empty lists), org.machanism.machai.project.layout.PythonProjectLayout (detects Python projects using pyproject.toml metadata; currently returns empty lists for sources/tests/docs), and org.machanism.machai.project.layout.DefaultProjectLayout (filesystem fallback that treats immediate subdirectories as potential modules excluding common tooling/build directories; returns empty lists for sources/tests/docs). A higher-level orchestrator, org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager, detects the applicable layout for a given project directory (Maven, Gradle, JS/TS, Python, or default), and org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor provides a base workflow to scan a project, recurse into modules when present, and delegate per-folder processing to subclasses. This library is intended for build tooling, plugins, and repository analysis tools that need reliable location of well-known folders across diverse projects and build systems.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Detects the appropriate ProjectLayout implementation for a project directory by checking for Maven (pom.xml), Gradle (build.gradle), JavaScript/TypeScript (package.json), Python (pyproject.toml heuristics), and falling back to a filesystem-based DefaultProjectLayout; throws if the directory does not exist.

examples.description: Detect a project layout for a given repository root.

features.name: scanFolder
features.description: Scans a project directory, detects its ProjectLayout, and either processes modules recursively (when getModules() returns non-null) or processes the root folder directly when modules are not reported by the layout.

examples.description: Implement a processor that uses detected layout paths while scanning a repository.

features.name: getModules
features.description: Returns module directories or names when the layout can infer nested modules (e.g., Maven modules when packaging=pom, Gradle child projects, npm/yarn workspaces, or immediate subdirectories for the default layout). Some implementations may return null to indicate that the project should be processed as a single root.

examples.description: Enumerate modules for a multi-module repository (when supported by the detected layout).

features.name: getSources
features.description: Returns conventional root-relative source directories for production code based on the detected build system or layout conventions (e.g., Maven build sourceDirectory/resources or Gradle src/main).

examples.description: Get production source roots for a Maven project.
